title: GKG unified query response
description: JSON payload returned by the query pipeline. All four query types (traversal, aggregation, path_finding, neighbors) produce this shape. Display metadata (labels, styles, domains) comes from the cached ontology, not from this response.

format_version.description: Semantic version of this response format (e.g. '1.0.0'). Major = breaking shape change, minor = new optional fields, patch = formatting bug fixes. Loaded from config/RAW_OUTPUT_FORMAT_VERSION.

query_type.description: Which query type produced this result. The frontend uses this to pick a default view (table for single-node traversal/aggregation, graph for multi-node traversal/path_finding/neighbors).

nodes.description: Deduplicated entity objects. Each entity appears once even if it participates in many edges. The frontend groups nodes by type for stacked table display and passes them to Three.js for graph rendering.

edges.description: Instance-level relationships between nodes. Each edge connects two specific nodes by their type and ID. Empty for single-node traversal and aggregation queries.

columns.description: Present for aggregation queries. Describes each computed column: its output name, the aggregation function used, the target node alias, and the property being aggregated. Lets the consumer distinguish aggregate values from entity properties without inspecting the ontology.

pagination.description: Present when the query included a cursor. Tells the agent whether more pages exist and how many total authorized rows are available.

type.description: Entity type name matching the ontology (e.g. User, MergeRequest, Project). Look up in the cached ontology for label_field, domain, and style.

id.description: Entity primary key, serialized as a string to avoid JavaScript precision loss for Int64 values exceeding 2^53-1. Together with type, forms the composite identity (e.g. User:42). Always included even if not selected.

additionalProperties.description: Entity properties selected by the query, plus any inlined aggregation values. Property names match ontology definitions.

from_id.description: Source node entity ID, serialized as a string.

to_id.description: Target node entity ID, serialized as a string.

type.description: Relationship type from the ontology (e.g. AUTHORED, MEMBER_OF, CONTAINS).

depth.description: For variable-length traversals (max_hops > 1), the hop count. Absent on single-hop edges.

path_id.description: For path finding queries, identifies which path this edge belongs to. Absent for non-path queries.

step.description: For path finding queries, the position of this edge within its path (0-indexed). Absent for non-path queries.

name.description: Output column name. Matches the key on the node where the aggregate value appears. Either the user-supplied alias or the function name (e.g. 'count').

function.description: Aggregation function applied (count, sum, avg, min, max, collect).

target.description: Node alias being aggregated over (e.g. 'u' for User). Absent when the function has no explicit target.

property.description: Property being aggregated (e.g. 'id'). Absent for count without a property.

value.description: Computed value for ungrouped (scalar) aggregations. Absent for grouped aggregations where values live on entity nodes.

has_more.description: True if more authorized rows exist beyond the current page.

total_rows.description: Total authorized rows before cursor slicing.
